title: Non-enzymatic passaging of hPSC
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1iaj7","text":"This protocol describes the procedure to passage hPSC culture using enzyme-free reagents. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4c650","text":"In this protocol, hPSC passaging is done in aggregate/clumps format. steps: Prepare coated tissue culture vessels and culture media according to hPSC line requirements and desired format. Refer to protocols: Coating of tissue Culture Vessels for hPSC and Maintenance of hPSC .
Aspirate and discard media from selected source vessel.
Wash the well once using DPBS (no Calcium/no Magnesium).
Keep prepared destination vessel at 37 °C until usage.
Add hPSC maintenance media per destination vessel, refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes. %s %s

Prepare required volume of the reagents and media according to the Table 1. Equilibrate the media to Room temperature . Culture media aliquot to be used can be warmed up at 37°C for 00:15:00 . However, to preserve recombinant proteins activity Room temperature equilibration is recommended.
Add enzyme-free reagent, refer to Table 1 for recommended working volumes.

Incubate the vessel at 37 °C monitoring hPSC detachment using a microscope. Refer to Table 2 for recommended incubation times. Cells will be ready to harvest when colonies start to be loosen though still attached. %s %s This incubation can also be done at Room temperature , recommendation at 37 °C is to avoid hPSC stress.
Add fresh hPSC maintenance media to the wells, refer to Table 1 for recommended harvesting volumes.

Pipette up and down using 5 mL serological pipette against the bottom of the well to dislodge cells from the culture surface. Repeated execution of this step leads to progressively smaller hPSC aggregates, which can increase cellular stress and promote cell death. Aim to maintain a balance that results in a uniform aggregate suspension - ideally with sizes ranging from approximately 50-200 µm while minimizing shear stress. The use of scrapers is generally not recommended. However, for hPSC cultures with strong adherence properties, employing a scraper may be preferable to prolonged incubation with enzyme-free dissociation reagents.

Collect hPSC aggregates suspension using 5 mL pipette and directly distribute drop-wise the approximate volume for splitting ratios: 1/10, 1/15, 1/20 (or as desired) to the destination vessel/s. Alternatively, aggregates suspension can be transferred to a conical tube prior distribution to better assess suspension volume.
Gently move destination vessels with freshly passed aggregates in cross shape to distribute them evenly.
Incubate the vessels at 37 °C and at 5 % volume CO 2 .
After 24:00:00 perform full media change. For further hPSC culture refer to protocol Maintenance of hPSC and protocol Reference pictures of hPSC cultured in defined conditions
Aspirate and discard coating solution from wells of destination vessel.
